replacement_target: later explicit XStack/AIDE extraction-review checkpoint or approved spinout checkpoint only

current_program_context.series_role: boundary mapping and extraction sequencing that must not compete with playable-baseline work

extraction_definition.document_first_extraction_readiness: Frozen portable contracts and clearly reusable semantics that can be promoted into an AIDE fact base before any code moves.
extraction_definition.later_code_extraction_candidacy: Live code that may eventually move or be mirrored into AIDE after baseline, stabilization, and bridge work.
extraction_definition.wrapping_vs_moving: Keep Dominium product, runtime, and control-plane code in place while later extracted portable cores talk through explicit adapters or wrappers.
extraction_definition.retaining_in_dominium: Keep the implementation in this repo because it is product-critical, runtime-critical, baseline-critical, or deeply tied to Dominium ownership.
extraction_definition.deprecating_rather_than_extracting: Do not spin the surface out; later convergence or retirement should reduce it instead.
extraction_definition.deferred_until_post_baseline: Extraction may be reviewed later, but only after the playable baseline exists and the current startup/session path is stabilized.

decision_class_definitions.extract_later_as_portable_core: Strongest future AIDE candidate. The extracted center is already document-first or code-light enough to justify later portable-core promotion after baseline.
decision_class_definitions.extract_later_with_wrapper_bridge: A reusable kernel may later move, but only if Dominium keeps product, runtime, or control-plane adapters behind.
decision_class_definitions.retain_in_dominium: Keep the surface in Dominium. AIDE may consume surrounding contract shapes, but this implementation stays repo-owned.
decision_class_definitions.deprecate_or_retire: Do not spin this out. Prefer later convergence, retirement, or historical retention over extraction.
decision_class_definitions.defer_until_after_baseline: Too baseline-critical, incomplete, or unstable to map beyond review later.
decision_class_definitions.mixed_needs_later_review: Surface contains real reusable and repo-bound portions at the same time.

ledger.name: Frozen portable AIDE contract artifacts
ledger.rationale: These artifacts already freeze the portable task, evidence/review, policy/permission, capability-profile, and adapter shapes without embedding runtime machinery.
extract_later_components: later schemas generated from the frozen contracts
portable validator or checker layer for the frozen fields
shim_requirements: optional bridge from AIDE contracts back into Dominium tooling
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
closure audit accepts the frozen contracts as stable
schema or export packaging is defined explicitly
unsafe_now_reasons: no standalone AIDE package exists yet
immediate value still comes from using these contracts inside Dominium

ledger.name: XStack core planner, scheduler, runner, and artifact substrate
ledger.rationale: This is the strongest live generic planner and runner substrate, and scripts/dev/gate.py plus tools/xstack/run.py already consume it as shared execution logic.
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
ControlX step taxonomy is stabilized
output and report path ownership is explicit
unsafe_now_reasons: the substrate is still wired into live repo gating and baseline assembly work

ledger.name: Low-level CompatX canonicalization and validation primitives
ledger.rationale: These helpers are the broadest reused XStack-adjacent substrate across runtime, client, server, release, trust, validation, and AppShell surfaces.
extract_later_components: canonical JSON text and hash helpers
shim_requirements: schema-root adapter for schema versus schemas
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
ownership-sensitive schema split remains explicit
contract export packaging is approved
unsafe_now_reasons: many baseline-critical surfaces currently import these helpers directly
the repo still owns the schema source landscape

ledger.name: CompatX profile checks and version-routing layer
ledger.rationale: The low-level contract primitives are reusable, but this layer is still tied to repo docs, repo schemas, version registries, and a migration path that currently stops at a migration stub.
extraction_prerequisites: migration story is no longer stub-only
policy ownership is explicit
schema or export adapter exists
unsafe_now_reasons: the layer still encodes repo-specific schema and doc expectations
migration behavior is incomplete

ledger.name: XStack ControlX orchestration surface
ledger.rationale: ControlX is a real orchestration surface, but it directly imports AuditX, CompatX, PerformX, PackagingX, registry compile, SessionX boot/create, TestX, and UI bind.
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
session and runtime surfaces are stabilized
Dominium step providers are cleanly declared
unsafe_now_reasons: ControlX coordinates many baseline-critical Dominium surfaces

ledger.name: Repo gate and review bridge scripts that consume XStack
ledger.rationale: These are repo bridges between XStack semantics and Dominium review and gate flows, not portable core.
extract_later_components: at most mapping hooks that let extracted cores feed repo-owned review and gate wrappers
shim_requirements: task and evidence or review adapters from extracted AIDE cores back into Dominium gate flows
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
X-9 closure audit confirms the retained gate posture
repo review surfaces are normalized
unsafe_now_reasons: these bridges are part of the repo operational discipline

ledger.name: Session create and pipeline-contract materialization
ledger.rationale: This surface is part of the shortest playable-baseline path and bakes in Dominium bundle, lock, session-template, and save-root assumptions.
extract_later_components: only document-first contract semantics already frozen elsewhere
shim_requirements: future profile and bundle mapping adapters instead of code movement
extraction_prerequisites: none for current planning because the implementation is retained
unsafe_now_reasons: it directly supports the baseline session path
it is still coupled to Dominium profile and save ownership

ledger.name: Session boot, SRZ, runtime, and control machinery
ledger.rationale: This cluster is deep runtime and session machinery, and tools/xstack/sessionx/runner.py still hardcodes the repo_root/saves/<save_id> layout while session_boot.py documents that same canonical path explicitly.
extract_later_components: at most later review of narrow reusable contracts already frozen by X-2 through X-6
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
save-root coupling is fixed
canonical startup path is stable
runtime ownership review is explicit
unsafe_now_reasons: the surface is baseline-critical
the current implementation is still fragile

ledger.name: UI bind checker and session-host boundary
ledger.rationale: The descriptor-validation side looks portable, but the checked host surface is still part of SessionX runtime and session control.
extraction_prerequisites: UI registry semantics stabilized
session host separated cleanly from runtime control
unsafe_now_reasons: the surface still straddles portable descriptor law and Dominium runtime implementation

ledger.name: TestX deterministic harness and suite corpus
ledger.rationale: The runner itself is reusable, but tools/xstack/testx/runner.py still depends on repo diff detection, semantic-impact helpers, the live suite corpus, and the .xstack_cache/xstack_testx cache root.
extraction_prerequisites: playable baseline exists
suite taxonomy and impact coverage are stable
report and output contract is explicit
unsafe_now_reasons: the harness is a live baseline gate
it is tightly coupled to this repo test corpus and Git layout

ledger.name: XStack AuditX and RepoX wrapper entrypoints
ledger.rationale: These are wrapper CLIs over repo-specific audit and review scans. auditx/check.py explicitly calls itself a wrapper, and repox/check.py hardcodes repo scan roots and review helpers.
shim_requirements: none for extraction because the wrappers themselves are not the future portable core
extraction_prerequisites: if later desired, portable rule schemas would need to be separated first
unsafe_now_reasons: wrapper behavior is not proof of portable ownership

ledger.name: SecureX minimal signature-status checks
ledger.rationale: The check surface is small and coherent, but it depends on Dominium pack loading, signature-status vocabulary, and trust policy resolution.
extract_later_components: narrow verifier semantics for signature-status classification
extraction_prerequisites: portable policy and permission linkage remains stable
trust-root provider boundary is explicit
unsafe_now_reasons: current behavior is still a Dominium trust and pack check

ledger.name: PerformX placeholder budget and fidelity checks
ledger.rationale: The file describes itself as a placeholder, reads build registries opportunistically, and emits warnings rather than a mature portable contract engine.
retain_components: current placeholder behavior until later Dominium cleanup or replacement
extraction_prerequisites: a real capability or policy evaluator would have to exist first
unsafe_now_reasons: placeholder status is not extraction readiness

ledger.name: Xi-7 and Xi-8 CI guardrail surface
ledger.rationale: The rule metadata is useful, but tools/xstack/ci/ci_common.py is deeply tied to Xi-series repo policy, GitHub workflow shape, audit report paths, and repo-local refusal categories.
extraction_prerequisites: closure audit confirms stable guardrail semantics
repo-specific rule IDs are separated from portable rule shapes
unsafe_now_reasons: the current surface is part of the Dominium repo policy spine

ledger.name: Unified validation engine plus legacy adapters
ledger.rationale: The validation engine is real and valuable, but it writes repo-local audit outputs, consumes many Dominium validators, and explicitly embeds a long legacy adapter bridge table.
extraction_prerequisites: baseline exists
legacy adapters are reduced or clearly ring-fenced
portable checker core is explicitly separated
unsafe_now_reasons: it is current repo validation infrastructure, not a clean portable core

ledger.name: Pack loading, contribution parsing, registry compile, and packaging pipeline
ledger.rationale: The registry compile compiler writes Dominium registry outputs and lockfiles, calls SessionX null-boot artifact helpers, and operates over live Dominium packs, bundles, release, and dist layout semantics.
extract_later_components: at most contract adapters around the frozen capability, policy, and adapter shapes
shim_requirements: future extracted cores should consume this through bundle, pack, and profile adapters instead of code movement
extraction_prerequisites: none for current planning because the implementation remains Dominium-owned
unsafe_now_reasons: it is core control-plane and baseline support substrate for the live repo

ledger.name: Python launcher/setup shells and shared AppShell command layer
ledger.rationale: The audit identifies these as the strongest repo-local operator shells for the playable baseline, and they encode repo-root, dist, profile, pack, trust, supervision, and product-command assumptions.
extract_later_components: only the portable contract shapes that the shells consume
shim_requirements: if later extracted cores need access, Dominium-owned operator wrappers should remain the integration boundary
extraction_prerequisites: none for current planning because these shells are baseline-critical Dominium surfaces
unsafe_now_reasons: baseline assembly explicitly depends on them as the canonical repo-local shells

ledger.name: AppShell and tool-level bridge helpers
ledger.rationale: These files are bridge hosts, not portable cores. They bind Dominium release, trust, packs, audit artifacts, and old import topology into current operator flows.
extract_later_components: only the abstract adapter or bridge contracts already frozen by X-3 through X-6
shim_requirements: these files are themselves the kind of bridge layer later extractions will need
extraction_prerequisites: none for current planning; later AIDE cores can target them as retained boundaries
unsafe_now_reasons: moving bridge code defeats the purpose of using Dominium-owned wrappers to protect baseline-critical behavior

ledger.name: Local singleplayer and authoritative loopback bridge
ledger.rationale: The audit calls this the strongest evidence-backed local authority path, and it is directly coupled to repo-local server boot, process spawn, loopback negotiation, and the current playable-baseline target.
extract_later_components: later extracted portable cores would integrate through it
shim_requirements: future adapters from extracted task, evidence, policy, or capability layers into Dominium runtime control
extraction_prerequisites: none for current planning because this remains baseline-critical Dominium runtime glue
unsafe_now_reasons: it is part of the current baseline runtime direction
it is still partially fragile

ledger.name: Release, update, and offline trust surfaces that consume XStack helpers
ledger.rationale: These surfaces import CompatX helpers, but they are still Dominium release and trust control-plane implementations tied to repo policy, install, dist, and publication semantics.
extract_later_components: only portable contract checking or hashing primitives already handled elsewhere
extraction_prerequisites: none for current planning because these are protected Dominium control-plane surfaces
unsafe_now_reasons: release and trust meaning is review-sensitive and explicitly protected

ledger.name: Legacy standalone ControlX and CompatX roots
ledger.rationale: These are older sibling roots that still provide bridge value, but the current semantic center for the XStack/AIDE series lives under tools/xstack/**.
retain_components: temporary bridge or archaeology value until explicit convergence work is approved
shim_requirements: compatibility aliases if future cleanup touches their consumers
extraction_prerequisites: post-baseline convergence task explicitly approved
unsafe_now_reasons: spinning out legacy siblings would freeze the wrong ownership center

ledger.name: Older XStack reference docs and repo-specific operating contract surfaces
ledger.rationale: The older docs are historical context only, and the Codex repo operating contract is intentionally specific to this repo build, validation, playtest, and directory-authority rules.
extraction_prerequisites: none
unsafe_now_reasons: these artifacts either describe superseded local context or intentionally govern this repo only

likely_aide_candidates.why: Strongest reusable planner and runner substrate.
Broadest reused contract primitives across XStack-adjacent consumers.
Reusable kernel likely exists once migration and schema-provider boundaries are explicit.
Runner semantics and sharding posture are reusable if suite and report adapters stay behind.
A later extracted orchestration kernel is plausible if Dominium step providers remain behind wrappers.
Narrow verifier semantics are plausible if trust and pack providers stay Dominium-owned.

definitely_dominium_retained.why: Baseline session materialization and save-tree ownership stay in Dominium.
Pack, registry, lockfile, dist, and packaging semantics are current Dominium control-plane substrate.
Canonical repo-local shells and AppShell product surfaces are baseline-critical.
These are retained integration seams, not portable cores.
Current loopback authority and local runtime path stay with Dominium.
Release and trust meaning is protected and repo-specific.
Repo-local operating law and historical reference docs are not AIDE assets.

mixed_or_deferred.why: Baseline-critical runtime path remains fragile and save-root coupled.
Portable descriptor checking and Dominium session host behavior still share the same surface.
Portable-looking rule metadata still mixes with Xi-specific repo policy.
Valuable checker infrastructure still owns repo suites, report sinks, and legacy adapter routing.
Repo gate and review bridges must remain stable during baseline assembly.

extraction_order_after_baseline.name: document_first_portable_core_promotion
scope: promote frozen AIDE task, evidence/review, policy/permission, capability-profile, and adapter contracts into a standalone AIDE contract pack
carry this extraction map as the boundary ledger
guardrails: do not weaken baseline ownership boundaries

extraction_order_after_baseline.name: low_level_portable_substrate_extraction
scope: extract low-level CompatX primitives: canonical_json, validator, schema_registry
guardrails: keep Dominium schema and provider adapters behind
do not move session, launcher, AppShell, or pack compile code

extraction_order_after_baseline.name: checker_and_harness_extraction_with_retained_bridges
scope: review a narrow wrapped subset of CompatX profile/version checks
review a narrow wrapped subset of TestX runner semantics
guardrails: do not move Dominium suites, registries, trust policy, or report sinks

extraction_order_after_baseline.name: orchestration_layer_extraction_with_provider_wrappers
scope: review whether ControlX profile and orchestration semantics can move behind Dominium step-provider adapters
guardrails: keep session/runtime, AppShell, pack compile, and product/operator shells in Dominium

extraction_order_after_baseline.name: optional_late_mixed_surface_review
guardrails: treat runtime/session/AppShell/release/trust surfaces as retained unless a later checkpoint authorizes more

doctrine_vs_implementation_vs_extraction: doctrinal similarity does not equal extraction readiness
implementation existence does not equal portability
extraction mapping does not equal immediate extraction execution
wrapper existence does not equal platform ownership
prohibited_extraction_shapes: moving mixed surfaces because they sound generic
treating wrapper code as portable core by default
using this map to justify immediate repo churn, broad renames, or code moves
claiming a separate AIDE runtime is ready now
collapsing document-first contract freezes, code extraction candidacy, and runtime implementation into one story
treating the current repo operating contract as AIDE policy
downstream_prompts_enabled: later post-baseline extraction checkpoints that need a conservative boundary map
future implementation prompts that need to know what to retain, wrap, defer, or retire before any code movement is proposed
